text: Planning a trip can be almost as exciting as the journey itself. When I visited Japan, I spent weeks researching destinations and local customs. Experiencing new cultures opened my eyes to different ways of life, from tranquil temples to bustling markets. The food was a highlight—I still remember the delicate taste of authentic ramen and fresh sushi. Language barriers were challenging; I often used hand gestures and translation apps to communicate. Despite the difficulties, these moments created lasting memories. Each interaction, whether a smiled greeting or a shared meal, enriched my understanding of the world. That trip taught me that travel is not just about seeing new places, but about connecting with people and embracing the unknown.
